summary: Implemented an explicit runtime-content-pack boot DTO path for sim-core and added an M2 boot-boundary contract test. The test compiles the M2 content-source fixture through content-runtime tooling, boots sim-core from the compiled runtime M2 pack, locks the deterministic initial WorldState hash at 42e3a2fe, and verifies schema-mismatched or bad-reference packs return structured errors without a runtime. It also scans authoritative sim-core source for content-source, Node fs, React, PixiJS, Electron, DOM, and real-time API usage.

tests_run.result: Initial status showed lead/orchestrator task-state files already modified plus one route message; implementation did not revert them.
7 test files passed; 35 tests passed. M2 boot-boundary contract included. Vitest emitted the existing MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning for tests/fixtures/deterministic-rng-worker.ts.
Architecture boundaries passed; self-test fixture produced 15 expected violations.
Node/worker hello hash 61e0ba1c matched; command/query hash d275faa4 matched; save/load hash 28074edd matched; save/load byte length 10920.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M2-BOOT-BOUNDARY-001
tests_run.result: M2-BOOT-BOUNDARY-001 JSON shape ok: IN_PROGRESS, R3, 6 acceptance criteria.
All workspace typecheck scripts passed, including sim-core, content-runtime, content-tools, web, desktop, save-format, UI, map-renderer, and platform.
1 protocol schema test file passed; 3 tests passed.
All matched files use Prettier code style after formatting the two new/changed implementation files.

decisions: Added a protocol-level runtime-content-pack boot DTO variant while preserving existing fixture boot inputs.
Kept sim-core independent of content-runtime imports by validating the runtime M2 pack as a plain DTO at the authority boundary.
Mapped M2 districts, settlements, and routes into existing WorldState v0 definitions only; no economy, population, route-choice, UI, platform-host hash, or broader product scope was added.
Used structured boot rejection errors with code/path/message; invalid pack rejection returns no SimulationRuntime.
risks: R3 public boot contract change requires systems_architect review.
The M2 content boot maps route baseTravelCost into existing RouteDefinition.lengthInMapUnits as a boundary placeholder until dedicated M2 transport systems are specified.
The contract test reads content-source only from test/tool side; sim-core source scan and architecture check guard against authority-layer imports.
requested_action: Review the runtime-content-pack boot DTO, sim-core M2 runtime pack validation boundary, deterministic hash fixture, and structured rejection behavior for R3 architecture acceptance.
